skill.name: nextjs-architecture
skill.description: Next.js architecture specialist. Use when designing Next.js applications, migrating to App Router, implementing Server Components, or optimizing Next.js performance. Covers App Router, Server Components, and best practices.
skill.summary: Plan Next.js App Router architecture, Server Component usage, migrations, and production performance work.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown fences, prose, folder examples, and TypeScript examples. No executable scripts, network calls, secret access, prompt injection, or malicious behavior were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences and documentation examples. They do not execute commands, call tools, or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain Markdown syntax inside SKILL.md, not Ruby, shell, or a runnable script. The file contains no command execution instructions or dynamic input handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Prose
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography alerts point to ordinary skill metadata, headings, and Next.js architecture prose. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, or security-sensitive implementation appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss descriptions, design, and architecture concepts. I found no crypto code, weak algorithm usage, or data protection workflow.

content.value_statement: Next.js projects often need clear choices around routing, rendering, data loading, and performance. This skill guides App Router architecture, Server Component patterns, migration planning, and production optimization.
actual_capabilities: Designs App Router folder structures and route organization.
Explains Server Component and Client Component boundaries.
Plans migrations from Pages Router to App Router.
Suggests rendering, streaming, and static generation strategies.
Reviews performance choices for production Next.js applications.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automated migration tools.
Does not inspect private repositories unless the assistant can access them.
Does not replace testing, profiling, or framework documentation review.
May need project-specific details for accurate architecture advice.

use_cases.title: Plan a New App Router Project
use_cases.description: Define the application structure, route groups, layouts, data loading model, and component boundaries before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Migrate an Existing Pages Router App
use_cases.description: Create a phased migration plan that maps pages, layouts, API routes, and data fetching into App Router patterns.

use_cases.title: Improve Production Performance
use_cases.description: Review rendering mode choices, streaming opportunities, image handling, code splitting, and static generation options.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Next.js Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Next.js project structure and suggest a clearer App Router layout. Focus on routes, layouts, and shared components.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Server and Client Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me decide which parts of this feature should be Server Components and which need Client Components. Explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Pages Router Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration plan from Pages Router to App Router for this application. Include routing, layouts, API routes, and testing steps.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Rendering Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Next.js architecture for production performance. Recommend rendering modes, caching, streaming, and bundle reduction changes.

output_examples.input: Design an App Router structure for a dashboard with authentication, analytics, and user settings.
output_examples.output: A route group plan for authenticated pages, shared dashboard layouts, nested analytics routes, and clear placement for user settings.

output_examples.input: Migrate a user profile page from Pages Router to App Router.
output_examples.output: A migration checklist covering page placement, layout conversion, parameter handling, data loading, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Improve the performance of a content-heavy Next.js site.
output_examples.output: A prioritized set of recommendations for static generation, incremental updates, streaming, image optimization, and bundle review.

best_practices: Share the current folder structure, routing model, and data fetching approach.
State performance goals, hosting assumptions, and user-facing latency concerns.
Ask for migration plans in phases when the application is already in production.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad architecture advice without describing the application domain.
Do not move everything to Client Components without a specific browser requirement.
Do not treat generated guidance as a substitute for running tests and profiling.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps design Next.js application architecture, App Router structures, Server Component boundaries, migrations, and performance strategies.

faq.question: Can it migrate code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides plans, patterns, and review guidance. Actual code edits require normal assistant access to the project files.

faq.question: Does it support Pages Router projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review Pages Router projects and plan migrations to App Router.

faq.question: Is it useful for full-stack Next.js apps?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers route handlers, layouts, data loading, rendering choices, and application structure.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guidance file and does not call external services.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
